title: Smoky Japanese Kombu Bisque
description: A light and savory bisque featuring charred kombu and melted butter, served in a mason jar.
instructions: Char the kombu sheets over an open flame or in a hot skillet for 2 minutes on each side.
In a large pot, melt the butter over medium heat and saute the sliced onion until softened.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute.
Add the charred kombu, chicken broth, water, sesame oil, salt, and black pepper to the pot.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 20 minutes.
Strain the bisque through a fine mesh sieve into a clean pot, discarding the solids.
Serve the bisque hot in mason jars, garnished with a sprinkle of sesame oil and a pinch of salt.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
